purpose: Guidance for the AI resume generation layer before creating replacements.json. This file should not be enforced by the DOCX patcher itself.

architecture_principles.separation_of_concerns: The Python patcher should only perform deterministic document conversion: master_resume.docx + replacements.json -> tailored_resume.docx.
architecture_principles.ai_layer_role: The AI/chatbot layer should make context-aware tailoring decisions using the job description, master resume, user preferences, and human-reader strategy before generating replacements.json.
architecture_principles.human_reader_priority: Optimize for real recruiters and hiring managers reading the resume before interviews, not only ATS keyword matching.
architecture_principles.chatbot_core: The SaaS product should keep a chatbot-style experience at its center because resume tailoring requires collaborative judgment, tradeoff discussion, and dynamic context awareness.
architecture_principles.explicit_usage_of_python_script: Always use the attached resume_patcher.py script for DOCX generation to ensure consistent formatting and avoid manual errors. Do not bypass the script by manually editing DOCX files.

avoid: flattening strong technical texture into bland corporate language
non-standard job titles that feel fabricated, such as 'Technical Implementation & Application Support Consultant' instead of 'Implementation Engineer' or 'Web Developer'
changing job titles for the sake of keyword matching when it creates a misleading employment history or feels like a fabrication
prioritize: evidence that a human recruiter can understand quickly
using 'Additional Web Development Experience' section instead of individual roles prior to Apica
preserving Apica role progression under a grouped employer header for SaaS support, application support, implementation, and professional services roles
strategic space allocation based on role relevance rather than a fixed bullet-count rule

bullet_preferences.note: Bullet counts are guidance for the AI generation layer, not hard validation rules for the Python patcher.
bullet_preferences.guidance: Use as many bullets as the resume strategy requires, while keeping the final resume readable and human-scannable. Avoid forcing compression solely to satisfy an arbitrary maximum.
bullet_preferences.preferred_recent_role_range: Usually 4-8 bullets when the role is relevant, but allow more when a single role is the primary proof for the target job.
bullet_preferences.preferred_older_role_range: Usually 0-3 bullets depending on relevance; collapse or remove details when older roles do not materially support the target job.
bullet_preferences.space_priority: Allocate resume space to the experience that best proves fit for the target role. Do not preserve low-value older-role bullets at the expense of highly relevant Apica or recent technical work.
avoid: compressing highly relevant experience into an unclear mega-block just to reduce bullet count
spending significant space on early-career roles when they are not important to the target role
treating any numeric bullet range as a hard rule

job_title_strategy.guidance: Functional title translation is acceptable when it accurately reflects the work and does not create a misleading employment history.
job_title_strategy.example: For Apica, 'Implementation Engineer' may be more useful and still credible than 'Professional Services Engineer' for implementation-focused roles. Avoid copying the exact target title if it feels fabricated.

content_strategy.summary: Make the hiring manager think this person could be a fit quickly; do not overload it with buzzwords.
content_strategy.skills: Tailor skill categories to the role while staying accurate. Include adjacent keywords only when defensible.
content_strategy.experience: Lead with the most relevant experience for the target role, but preserve credibility, chronology, progression, and current activity. Avoid merging role progressions when the progression itself is a selling point.
content_strategy.older_roles: Compress older roles aggressively when they do not support the target role, usually into Additional Web Development Experience rather than individual full entries.
content_strategy.cover_letters: Use cover letters to explain motivation, fit, and unusual alignment rather than restating the resume.
content_strategy.apica: For primary-lane SaaS support/application support/implementation roles, preserve Apica as a grouped employer with individual role progression unless the user explicitly requests a compressed format.
content_strategy.recent_work: Use recent Pearl/Freelance work to answer what the user has been doing lately, but keep it compact when Apica is the stronger target-role proof.

default_positioning.primary_lane: Technical/Application Support, Implementation, Professional Services, and customer-facing SaaS systems work.
default_positioning.secondary_lane: WordPress/WooCommerce and custom web systems should be framed as implementation, product operations, troubleshooting, and workflow ownership when appropriate, not merely coding.
default_positioning.preferred_framing: Support Engineer+ / implementation-focused technical operator who can bridge customers, systems, and engineering.

ats_strategy.guidance: Use role-relevant keywords naturally in summary, skills, and bullets. Do not sacrifice human credibility for exact keyword stuffing.
ats_strategy.exact_title_matching: Can help, but should be balanced against human smell-test risk.

output_expectations.docx_formatting: Preserve the master resume's formatting and structure as closely as possible.
output_expectations.json_generation: Generate clean replacements.json that the patcher can apply deterministically.
output_expectations.review_step: Render-check the DOCX when possible before delivery.
output_expectations.strategic_structure: Formatting preservation matters, but should not override role-relevant structure such as grouped Apica progression or compact older-role handling.

experience_structure_strategy.avoid_overcorrection: Do not solve one structural problem by creating another. Preserve the strongest human-reader signals: relevance, chronology, progression, and current activity.

apica_grouped_progression.default_for_primary_lane_roles: For Technical Support, Application Support, Customer Support Engineering, Professional Services, Implementation, and customer-facing SaaS systems roles, do not compress Apica into one undifferentiated role.
apica_grouped_progression.preferred_structure: Use a grouped Apica employer header showing total tenure and progression, then keep Professional Services Engineer, Senior Technical Support Engineer, and Technical Support Engineer as separate role entries underneath.
apica_grouped_progression.gold_header_purpose: The grouped Apica header should communicate 6 years of enterprise SaaS support/professional services experience and internal progression without replacing the individual role entries.
apica_grouped_progression.role_progression_priority: Preserve the user's progression at Apica because it is a major credibility signal.
apica_grouped_progression.bullet_distribution: Distribute bullets by relevance to the target job. For SaaS support roles, Senior Technical Support Engineer and Professional Services Engineer usually deserve the most detail; Technical Support Engineer should usually be shorter but still present.
apica_grouped_progression.do_not: Do not merge all Apica experience into one large block unless the user explicitly asks for a compressed one-company format.

recent_contract_work.purpose: Answer the human-reader question of what the user has been doing lately.
recent_contract_work.guidance: Keep Pearl/Freelance/Subscription Everything visible and credible, but usually compact for SaaS support roles unless the target job strongly rewards WordPress, WooCommerce, web development, product operations, or plugin troubleshooting.
recent_contract_work.do_not: Do not let current contract work hijack the resume narrative away from the primary lane when Apica is more relevant.

older_roles.default_handling: Collapse pre-Apica roles into a compact Additional Web Development Experience section unless the target role specifically rewards early web development, WordPress, PHP, JavaScript, C#/.NET, or software engineering experience.
older_roles.individual_role_exception: Keep an early role as an individual entry only when it directly supports the target role or provides unusually strong keyword/credibility alignment.
older_roles.space_priority: Early-career roles should not consume significant page space when Apica or recent technical work is more relevant.
older_roles.preferred_format: Use compact company/title/date lines, optionally with one short combined description if useful.

default_ordering_for_primary_lane_roles: Relevant Enterprise SaaS Experience with grouped Apica header and individual Apica role progression
